title: Diagnostics
rules: use short timestamps with time only
keep log output structured and compact
do not depend on console as primary sink
procedure: Create or open session log file.

primary_when: the request is specifically about diagnostics initialization, session logging, or failure reporting
the request is about setting up runtime trace output or startup visibility
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about UI rendering, performance tuning, or assembly parsing

solves: how to make startup and runtime failures visible early
how to keep diagnostics available even when console access is weak or absent
how to record compact runtime traces without bloated infrastructure

core_principles: Diagnostics should exist before optional runtime systems start.
Primary diagnostics output should be structured, compact, and file-backed.
Failure visibility matters more than logging feature breadth.
constraints: Diagnostics setup must be available early in startup.
Log output should remain compact enough for repeated runtime sessions.
Failure reporting paths must not depend on optional subsystems.
decision_rules: Use diagnostics as the owner when visibility, trace output, or failure reporting dominates the task.
Prefer file-backed logging when console presence is unreliable or secondary.
Keep diagnostics interfaces minimal so they can initialize early and stay available under failure.

preferred_patterns: minimal logging interface exposed to the rest of the runtime
avoid_patterns: late diagnostics initialization after optional systems
heavy diagnostics dependencies in early bootstrap

recipes.name: Bring up session diagnostics early
recipes.when: Use when startup visibility and failure traces matter from the first runtime phase.
steps: Open or create the log before optional systems start.
Bind a compact formatter and writer.
Record startup milestones and failures explicitly.

pitfalls: Relying on console output as the only diagnostic sink.
Initializing diagnostics too late to capture startup failures.
Making early diagnostics depend on optional infrastructure.
review_checklist: Is the primary sink file-backed or otherwise durable?
Can failure paths log without optional systems being alive?

references.title: Local Reference: Framework Diagnostics
references.interpretation: Use this first for early diagnostics policy, file-backed logging, and failure visibility rules.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for diagnostics setup and runtime trace design.

references.title: Local Reference: Framework Bootstrap
references.interpretation: Use this to align diagnostics startup timing with the broader bootstrap sequence.
references.why: Use to place diagnostics correctly in the startup order.
